candidate_problems.text: How can 'epistemic progress' be operationalized independently of raw predictive performance, so the conjecture becomes testable rather than rhetorical? The core unresolved tension is whether better conjecture-criticism-retention loops produce measurable knowledge growth that is not just another form of benchmark optimization or hidden scaling. Without a clear metric, 'structure' may collapse back into substrate proxies.

What specific structural mechanisms actually yield cumulative error-correction in AI systems, and under what conditions do they outperform additional scale? The conjecture asserts that selection, criticism, memory, and abstraction are now the main bottlenecks, but it leaves open which designs matter most, how they interact as a system, and whether their gains persist once compared against simply training a larger model.

Is there a principled tradeoff or complementarity between statistical absorption and explicit epistemic structure, or are they ultimately different expressions of the same underlying capability? The conjecture depends on a real distinction between substrate-bound improvement and structural improvement, but it remains unresolved whether this is a deep ontological divide or just a temporary engineering decomposition that disappears at sufficient scale.
